skill.name: meta-results-forest-plot-analyzer
skill.description: Analyzes forest plots for meta-analysis using a Vision LLM, generating detailed statistical descriptions and formatting figure legends in Chinese or English. Includes Python scripts for consistent output formatting with citation markers and structured headers.
skill.summary: Analyze forest plot images and format meta-analysis figure legends with statistical interpretation
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated all 44 static findings and determined every one is a false positive. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts matched normal text strings such as license identifiers and metadata hash values, not actual cryptographic code. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' alerts matched Markdown inline code formatting (backtick-enclosed text like `scripts/format_result.py`), not executable backtick syntax. The 'System reconnaissance' alerts matched benign documentation text. The 'Hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md:7 is a reference link to the skill's own GitHub repository with no active network requests. The Python scripts perform only local text formatting with no network access, file writes, subprocess calls, or eval-like execution. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown inline code formatting misidentified as shell backtick execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 23 backtick patterns in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. Confidence: 0.98 (Very High). All occurrences are Markdown inline code formatting used to highlight command names, file paths, and parameter values. No actual shell backtick execution syntax exists in the file. This is a confirmed false positive from pattern matching on backtick characters in documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Normal text misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 28 occurrences as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' across all four files. Confidence: 0.99 (Very High). These match ordinary text including license strings (MIT), hash values in audit JSON metadata, and descriptive phrases. No cryptographic primitives, hashing functions, or encryption code exist anywhere in the skill. This is a confirmed false positive from overly broad pattern matching.

dangerous_patterns.title: Reference URL to source repository
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md:7 contains a hardcoded URL pointing to the skill's own GitHub repository as a source attribution link. Confidence: 0.90 (Very High). This is a static reference link in markdown, not an active network request. No data is sent to this URL. The Python scripts make no network calls. Risk is negligible.

content.user_title: Analyze Forest Plots and Generate Meta-Analysis Figure Legends
content.value_statement: Researchers and clinicians need to interpret forest plots from meta-analyses but manual description writing is time-consuming and inconsistent. This skill uses vision AI to analyze forest plot images and automatically generates formatted figure legends with statistical details in English or Chinese.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes forest plot images using a Vision LLM to describe key statistical details including heterogeneity (I²), P-values, and effect sizes
Formats output text by inserting citation markers such as (Figure 2) before the final punctuation mark
Appends structured figure legends with language-specific headers in English or Chinese
Appends a placeholder for inserting the forest plot image into the formatted output
Generates descriptions exceeding 300 words with study counts and sample size reporting when visible
Validates the execution environment using a helper script to confirm script availability before running the main workflow
limitations: Requires a forest plot image as input and cannot generate plots from raw numerical data alone
Statistical interpretation quality depends on the Vision LLM and may miss details in low-resolution or complex images
Only supports English and Chinese output languages with no option to add other languages without modifying the script
The formatting script uses a fixed figure number of 2 and does not support dynamic figure numbering across documents

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Author
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a systematic review needs to interpret pooled effect sizes from forest plots and generate publication-ready figure legends with proper statistical reporting.
use_cases.target_user: Academic researchers and systematic review authors

use_cases.title: Clinical Guideline Developer
use_cases.description: A clinician developing evidence-based guidelines needs to quickly interpret meta-analysis results and produce formatted summaries for guideline documents in Chinese or English.

use_cases.title: Medical Student Learning Meta-Analysis
use_cases.description: A medical student learning to read forest plots needs detailed descriptions that explain heterogeneity, confidence intervals, and statistical significance in a structured format.
use_cases.target_user: Medical students and trainees in evidence-based medicine

prompt_templates.title: Basic Forest Plot Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this forest plot image. Describe the effect sizes, heterogeneity (I²), and whether the overall result is statistically significant.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a forest plot image and wants a basic statistical interpretation

prompt_templates.title: Analysis with Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this forest plot. Title: 'Effect of Drug A on Blood Pressure'. Statistics: I²=45%, P=0.03. Outcome: Systolic BP reduction. Language: English. Describe in detail and format the figure legend.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides study metadata alongside the forest plot image for richer analysis

prompt_templates.title: Chinese Language Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this forest plot image in Chinese. Include heterogeneity statistics, P-value, number of studies, sample sizes, and a conclusion on statistical significance. Format with the standard figure legend.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs the analysis output in Chinese for a Chinese-language publication

prompt_templates.title: Batch Analysis with Script Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: First validate the execution environment by running the validation script. Then analyze these three forest plot images from my meta-analysis. For each one, describe the pooled effect, heterogeneity, and significance. Format all outputs with consistent figure legends using the formatting script.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user processing multiple forest plots and validating the toolchain before execution

output_examples.input: Forest plot image showing pooled effect of a drug intervention with I²=50%, P=0.02, 8 studies, English output
output_examples.output: Forest Plot header followed by a detailed paragraph describing the eight included studies, the pooled effect size direction, moderate heterogeneity (I²=50%), statistically significant result (P=0.02), with (Figure 2) citation inserted before the final period, and a formatted figure legend appended below a placeholder image tag.

output_examples.input: Forest plot with high heterogeneity I²=85%, non-significant P=0.34, Chinese output
output_examples.output: Chinese-language header followed by a detailed Chinese description noting the substantial heterogeneity across studies, the non-significant pooled result, individual study variations, and concluding with the (Figure 2) marker and a Chinese figure legend with image placeholder.

best_practices: Provide the forest plot image along with any known metadata such as study title, I² value, and P-value to improve analysis accuracy
Validate the execution environment using the helper script before running the full workflow to catch path issues early
Review the generated statistical description for accuracy against the source image since Vision LLM interpretation may vary with image quality
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to generate forest plots from raw data tables as it only analyzes existing plot images
Do not rely on the skill to determine clinical significance as it only reports statistical measures from the visible plot content
Do not skip the script-based formatting step as raw LLM output lacks proper citation markers and structured figure legends

faq.question: What types of forest plots can this skill analyze?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes standard forest plots commonly found in meta-analysis publications. It works best with clear, high-resolution images showing individual study effect sizes, confidence intervals, and pooled results.

faq.question: Does the skill require an image file or can it work with a description?
faq.answer: The skill is designed to analyze forest plot images using a Vision LLM. If you provide only a text description without an image, the analysis quality will be limited.

faq.question: What languages does the formatted output support?
faq.answer: The formatting script supports English and Chinese output. The header, footer, and figure legend are automatically adjusted based on the language you specify.

faq.question: Can I change the figure number from 2 to something else?
faq.answer: The current script uses a fixed figure number of 2. To use a different number, you would need to edit the format_result.py script directly.

faq.question: What statistical measures does the analysis include?
faq.answer: The analysis describes heterogeneity (I²), P-values, effect sizes, number of studies, sample sizes when visible, and concludes on overall statistical significance.

faq.question: Do I need Python installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the formatting step requires Python 3.10 or later to run the format_result.py script. The validation helper also requires Python.
